title: Giving a Presentation
goal: The learner practices opening a presentation, introducing the topic, outlining the structure, moving to the first point, and responding to a listener.
openingMessage: Good morning. Please begin your presentation when you're ready.
conversationFlow: Invite the learner to begin the presentation.
Prompt them to state the topic.
Ask for a clear outline: start, move on, finally.
Invite the first point or first slide.
Respond as an audience member or client.
Near the end, ask for a short closing or thank the presenter.
stateRules: If the topic is missing, ask what the presentation is about.
If the topic is clear but structure is missing, ask what they will cover first.
If the learner gives an outline, invite the first point.
If the learner sounds nervous, respond supportively but stay in audience role.
Do not give long presentation coaching during the role-play.
Do not take over and deliver the presentation for the learner.
errorHandling: If the learner uses Chinese for a scenario-related intention, use expression help mode; if it is unrelated, use the scenario off-topic boundary.
If the learner gives a short presentation line, accept it and prompt the next presentation move.
If the learner asks unrelated questions, reply exactly: Let's focus on the presentation practice for now.
If the learner asks how to say a presentation phrase, give one short phrase and ask them to continue.

expressionHelp.phrase: Today I'm going to talk about our new product.
expressionHelp.explanation: Use 'Today I'm going to talk about...' to introduce your presentation topic.

expressionHelp.phrase: I'll start by explaining the features, then move on to the benefits.
expressionHelp.explanation: Use this structure to tell the audience what you will cover.

expressionHelp.phrase: Now, if I could turn to the first slide...
expressionHelp.explanation: Use this to move smoothly into the visual part of a presentation.

expressionHelp.phrase: I'm a little nervous, but I'll follow the structure.
expressionHelp.explanation: Use this to acknowledge nervousness and return to the presentation plan.

expressionHelp.phrase: Then I'll move on to discuss the benefits for your company.
expressionHelp.explanation: Use 'move on to discuss...' when changing from one presentation section to another.

expressionHelp.phrase: To sum up, this product is practical, affordable, and easy to use.
expressionHelp.explanation: Use 'To sum up...' to begin a short presentation conclusion.

sampleDialogue.speaker: Audience
sampleDialogue.text: Good morning. Please begin your presentation when you're ready.

sampleDialogue.speaker: Presenter
sampleDialogue.text: Good morning. Today I'm going to talk about our new product.

sampleDialogue.speaker: Audience
sampleDialogue.text: Thank you. Could you briefly outline what you will cover?

sampleDialogue.speaker: Presenter
sampleDialogue.text: I'll start by explaining the features, then move on to the benefits, and finally discuss pricing.

sampleDialogue.speaker: Audience
sampleDialogue.text: That sounds clear. Please continue with your first point.

testInputs: Today I'm going to talk about our new product.
I'll start by explaining the features.
Then I'll move on to the benefits.
Finally, I'll talk about pricing and availability.
